company_type: Technology - Data Center and Cloud Networking
segments: Core (Data Center, Cloud and AI Networking)

supply_chain.dependencies: Primarily reliant upon predominant merchant silicon vendor Broadcom for switching chips. Single-source and limited-source suppliers for key components including integrated circuits and power supplies. No guaranteed supply contracts with component suppliers.
supply_chain.risks: Supply chain shortages and extended lead times. Sole-source dependency on Broadcom for merchant silicon. Manufacturing concentrated in Malaysia, Vietnam, Mexico. Geopolitical risks affecting manufacturers and suppliers including US-China trade tensions, tariffs, Russia-Ukraine conflict, Israel-Hamas conflict, Houthi attacks on Red Sea shipping. China controls on certain metals used in semiconductor manufacturing (gallium, germanium, tungsten, tellurium, bismuth, molybdenum, indium). Supplier consolidation risks.

regulatory_risks: US export and import controls restricting sales to China and other countries
Enhanced US tax, tariff, and import/export restrictions
US government tariffs on goods from China, Mexico, Canada (25% tariffs scheduled on Mexican products, additional 10% on Chinese products)
Retaliatory tariffs from China, Mexico, Canada on US goods
US Foreign Corrupt Practices Act compliance
California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA) and California Privacy Rights Act (CPRA)

State privacy laws in Connecticut, Virginia, Colorado, Utah, Florida, Montana, Oregon, Texas, Delaware, Tennessee, Iowa, Maryland, Minnesota, New Hampshire, Nebraska, New Jersey, Indiana, Rhode Island, Kentucky
India Digital Personal Data Protection Act 2023
US government restrictions on semiconductors and semiconductor manufacturing equipment to China
China export controls on critical metals (gallium, germanium, tungsten, tellurium, bismuth, molybdenum, indium)
China restrictions on use of Micron products
AI regulatory requirements in UK, EU, and Asia-Pacific countries
Government procurement regulations requiring domestic manufacturing or domestic origin components

sanction_risks: US sanctions against Russia following Ukraine invasion
Economic sanctions against Russia and Belarus
US export controls on China for semiconductors and AI technology
Potential sanctions on China if Taiwan-China relations deteriorate
US tariffs on Chinese goods (existing tariffs of 7.5%, 10%, 15%, 25%, plus additional 10% imposed February 2025)
US tariffs on Mexican goods (25% scheduled, temporarily deferred)
US tariffs on Canadian goods (temporarily deferred)
Retaliatory Chinese tariffs and export controls on critical metals
Potential extreme tariff escalation if affected countries implement retaliatory measures
Restrictions on sales to entities on US restricted party lists
Worldwide licensing requirements for certain integrated circuits used in AI model training
China unreliable entity list potentially limiting business with Chinese customers
